channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸🛡️ US plans major cut to fighter jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
item.description: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, the New York Times reported, citing two senior European officials. The move comes as the Trump administration shifts focus away from European defense.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump raising expectations that this time he really will close deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump is anticipating a significant weekend for his presidency, with the World Cup returning to the U.S. and potential progress on an Iran peace deal that could wind down the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚖️ Palestine Action activists to be sentenced as terrorists over UK raid on Israel defense firm
item.description: Four activists convicted of criminal damage at an Elbit Systems factory near Bristol will be sentenced as terrorists in what is believed to be a legal first in the UK. The ruling means they will not qualify for early release provisions.

item.title: 📉💰 Wall Street confronts newly led Federal Reserve as rate hike fears rattle markets
item.description: A suddenly rocky U.S. stock market confronts a potential wildcard next week: A newly led Federal Reserve at a time investors are worried that interest rate hikes to fight inflation could dampen enthusiasm for equities.

item.title: 🚀💰 SpaceX launches $2 trillion market debut, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk's space and AI company went public in one of the biggest IPOs in history, with shares opening at $150 and pushing SpaceX's valuation above $2 trillion as investors bet on Musk's sprawling empire spanning rockets to AI.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Expects Lebanon Inclusion in Any US-Iran Deal
item.description: A leading Hezbollah politician expressed confidence that Iran will insist on Lebanon being included in any ceasefire agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a diplomatic resolution to the Gulf conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Stock Markets Hit Two-Month High on Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Stock markets in the United Arab Emirates surged Friday as regional hopes grew for a potential ceasefire deal between Iran and the United States that could end months of conflict in the Gulf.

item.title: 🚀 SpaceX Makes Historic Stock Market Debut After Record IPO
item.description: SpaceX began trading on Nasdaq after the largest initial public offering in history, with shares opening at $150 and Elon Musk becoming the worlds first trillionaire as investors backed his vision spanning rockets and orbital AI.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Air Force Warns of High-Probability Russian Missile Launch
item.description: Ukraine's Air Force warned of a high probability of a Russian missile launch from the Oreshnik site within the next day, expected to come from the Kapustin Yar launch site in southern Russia.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China-Russia Border River Tests Beijing's Ties With North Korea
item.description: A tiny river on Russia's border is testing China's relationship with Kim Jong-un, as Beijing seeks Sea of Japan access that neither China nor Pyongyang have publicly acknowledged.

item.title: 🤖 Ukraine's Defense AI Chief Predicts 'New Paradigm' of Warfare
item.description: Ukraine's defense AI chief says the system that possesses more data and better understands it will gain advantage, predicting a fundamental shift in how warfare is conducted.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq Foils Plot to Assassinate National Security Service Chief
item.description: Iraqi security forces foiled a plot to assassinate the National Security Service chief and senior officers, arresting suspects linked to a banned Baath Party front group.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Agrees to Performance-Based Deal Requiring Major Concessions
item.description: Iran has agreed to a performance-based deal that requires Tehran to destroy its nuclear material and dismantle its nuclear program, with none of its money released until compliance is verified.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia's $8 Million Visa Scandal Threatens Talent and Investor Drive
item.description: A major visa scandal involving $8 million threatens Indonesia's drive to attract talent and investors, as public-sector corruption remains an 'open secret' in the country.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Picks Wall Street's 'Top Cop' as New US Spy Chief
item.description: Trump selected Jay Clayton, former SEC chair known as Wall Street's 'top cop,' as the new US spy chief after facing backlash over his initial choice of Pulte.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Unveils $67.5B Budget as Economy Reaches Record $452B
item.description: Pakistan unveiled an 18.77 trillion-rupee budget for fiscal year 2026-27 as its economy reached a record $452 billion, with proposals for higher development spending.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Denies Making Major Concessions to Iran, Says Leaked Deal Terms Are Untrue
item.description: President Trump pushed back against reports that the US had made significant concessions to Iran in peace negotiations, with a senior official calling any emerging pact performance-based with Tehran receiving no frozen assets until it fulfills its commitments.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Fall to Near Two-Month Lows as Trump Cancels Planned Strikes on Iran
item.description: Crude dropped nearly 3% to its lowest level in nearly two months after President Trump called off new strikes on Iran, reducing fears of escalation following tit-for-tat attacks earlier in the week.

item.title: 🇺🇦 US Senate Seeks $750 Million for Ukraine in $1.15 Trillion Defense Bill
item.description: The massive Senate defense bill supports $1.15 trillion for total defense spending and includes $750 million in assistance for Ukraine, determining everything from ships and aircraft purchases to troop pay raises and addressing geopolitical threats.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran, Israel and US Remain Locked in Low-Intensity Conflict Despite Nominal Cease-Fire
item.description: Two months after announcing a cease-fire, all three sides remain in a dangerous limbo between peace and war, with ongoing low-intensity operations and unresolved tensions over the nuclear program.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Leaders to Discuss Response to Second China Shock at Upcoming Summit
item.description: Europe faces a new economic jolt from China driven by three distinct policies, with the $1.2 trillion trade surplus inspiring both envy and fear among EU leaders meeting next week to coordinate responses.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Switzerland to Vote on Population Cap in Referendum with Europe-Wide Implications
item.description: Swiss voters decide Sunday on capping population at 10 million in a referendum compared to Brexit for its potential economic and EU relations impact. The measure would require reducing immigration and could strain bilateral agreements with the EU.

item.title: 🚀 SpaceX Makes Historic Stock Market Debut After Record $75 Billion IPO
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq following the largest IPO in history. The company priced shares at $135, making it one of the world's most valuable companies as investors clamor for access to the space and satellite business.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesian Students Protest Government Policies Amid Economic Strain
item.description: Hundreds of students rallied in Jakarta against President Prabowo Subianto's spending priorities and fuel price hikes. Demonstrators criticized subsidies and free meals programs as the country faces growing economic pressures.

item.title: 🛡️ NATO Weighs New Defense Plans as US Plans Reductions for European Operations
item.description: NATO's top military officer is considering alternative plans to defend Europe if Russia attacks, following reported US cuts to fighter jets and warships for NATO operations. The moves come as European nations race to bolster defense capacities.
